sprint: Sprint 13 -- Pi Run Phase: First Real Data
backlogItem: B-037 (Run phase) + B-043 (auto-sync building block) + B-045 (test harness)

testBaseline.note: Baseline from Sprint 12 close (4/4 stories merged to main@ccb47f2). 2145 Windows fast-suite. Pi aarch64 baseline 1583 + 47 pi_only. Sprint 12 also shipped post-merge fixes 7051ebb (chi-srv-01 IP .120 â†’ .10), d93db32+3aaa5bd (lgpio + apt deps), 74efbdb (jinja2 server dep), a5f21d2+7dcf202 (validate_pi_to_server.sh hang fix; superseded by B-045 / US-191).

sprintNotes: CLOSEOUT 2026-04-19 (Session 23) — MILESTONE: First real Eclipse OBD-II data persisted to local SQLite. BL-006 (Sprint blocked on CIO+car) RESOLVED via live PM+CIO bench drill (engine in garage, KOEO→running idle). Sprint result: 4/5 → 2 formal pass + 2 bench-proven (technical chain WIN, engineering deliverables carry to Sprint 14) + 1 blocked on TD-024. Highlights: OBDLink LX paired/bonded/trusted; /dev/rfcomm0 bound channel 1; python-obd handshake → 'Car Connected | ISO 9141-2 | ELM327 v1.4b'; 11 PIDs live-queried (5 sweeps cold-start, then orchestrator main.py captured 149 rows in 60s warm-idle); ECU witnessed cold→closed-loop transition (LTFT=0.00% — tune dialed; STFT oscillating ±1.5%; O2 0V↔0.82V switching; timing 5-9°; coolant 73-74°C; RPM stabilized 793 avg). TWO BUGS surfaced: TD-023 OBD connection layer treats macAddress as serial-port path (workaround: set OBD_BT_MAC=/dev/rfcomm0; restored to MAC at session close); TD-024 pi.hardware.status_display crashes with GL BadAccess under X11, kills orchestrator runLoop at 0.6s (US-170 deferred to Sprint 14). ADDENDUM (Session 23, post-shutdown push): CIO directed PM to attempt end-to-end milestone push. sync_now.py crashed on TD-025 (sync assumes id col on every in-scope table; vehicle_info uses vin PK, calibration_sessions uses session_id PK). PM bypassed via direct client.pushDelta() per-table loop and successfully delivered 176 rows to chi-srv-01:8000 (149 realtime_data + 11 statistics + 16 connection_log; profiles errored on TD-026 — int() cast fails on TEXT 'daily' PK). END-TO-END MILESTONE COMPLETE: real Eclipse OBD-II data is now on the server. TD-025 + TD-026 owed.
GOAL: Pi mounted in the Eclipse, BT-paired with the OBDLink LX (MAC 00:04:3E:85:0D:FB), reading real engine idle data from the ECU, displaying it correctly. Done-milestone for B-037 Run phase.

AGENT MODEL: Hybrid (same as Sprint 10/11/12). Ralph writes code on Windows + SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.28 for Pi work. CIO physically does: mount Pi in car, plug OBDLink LX into car OBD-II port, start engine, BT-pair handshake, eyeball the display.

PI STATE ON SPRINT START: Sprint 12 shipped 4/4 + post-merge hygiene. Pi runs systemd eclipse-obd in --simulate mode (still). HardwareManager + UpsMonitor + GPIO button live. python-OBD library resolves correctly post US-187 rename. Pygame renders to OSOYOO 3.5" via X11 (DISPLAY=:0 + XAUTHORITY required â€” Session 21 surprise; baked into US-170 + render scripts this sprint).
SERVER STATE ON SPRINT START: chi-srv-01:8000 healthy at 10.27.27.10. No server changes this sprint.
SPRINT-BRANCH WORKFLOW (Rule 8): runs on sprint/pi-run (created by PM from main@1b740e3). Marcus committed this setup. Ralph leaves all code unstaged; Marcus commits at sprint close + merges to main.

DECISION CAPTURED 2026-04-18 (PM): Sprint 13 = 5 stories (2M + 3S). Tighter than Sprint 11 (7 stories) deliberately â€” Run phase has hardware unknowns (BT can be finicky, first real OBD reads may surface PID-support quirks specific to the Eclipse's ECU). US-169 (UPS in-car ignition cycles) and the B-043 follow-on US-189/US-190 are explicitly Sprint 14 candidates.

EXECUTION ORDER: US-167 BT pairing FIRST â€” gateway story, US-168 + US-170 wait on it. While US-167 is in flight, US-188 (WiFi detection, bench-only) and US-191 (flat-file replay harness, bench-only) can progress in parallel on Windows. Once US-167 lands â†’ US-168 (live idle data) â†’ US-170 (display).

FROM TONIGHT'S BENCH PROOF (Session 21, 2026-04-18): three pre-car checks all PASS. Sync e2e proven (523 rows pushed Pi â†’ server, lastSync timestamp updated). HDMI render proven (6 gauges visible on OSOYOO via X11). UPS power-source detection proven (2 unplug/replug cycles, VCELL trend caught every transition in ~2-4s). The car move is unblocked.

BUNDLED HOUSEKEEPING (small fixes, NOT full stories â€” Ralph touches as he goes): (a) API_KEY env wire-up properly (Sprint 11 leak â€” server .env + Pi .env need matching key, deploy scripts pass-through). (b) OBD_BT_MAC config default â€” sync_now.py choked on missing default; needs sane fallback. (c) pygame DISPLAY/XAUTHORITY in render scripts AND in eclipse-obd.service [Service] block (US-170 covers the service-file part). (d) Multi-HDMI dev workflow note â€” xrandr forces output for bench dev (in-car single-display moot).
STOP EARLY IF SSH OR BT BREAKS. Any story depending on Pi SSH must verify connectivity first. US-167 BT pairing has its own STOP conditions â€” see story body.

stories.title: Bluetooth OBD-II pairing with OBDLink LX (gateway story)
stories.intent: First-time pair the OBDLink LX (MAC 00:04:3E:85:0D:FB) to the Pi. Establish a serial port binding (/dev/rfcomm0). python-OBD's obd.OBD() constructor connects via that serial port. Pairing survives reboot. Config-driven (MAC in config.json, never hardcoded â€” per B-044 standing rule). GATEWAY story â€” US-168, US-170, all Run-phase work waits on this.

scripts/pair_obdlink.sh (new â€” CIO-runnable BT pairing driver)
src/pi/obdii/obd_connection.py (if connection logic needs BT-specific tuning)
src/pi/obdii/config/loader.py or similar (read OBD_BT_MAC from config)
config.json (pi.bluetooth section already exists; verify defaults)
.env.example (OBD_BT_MAC entry + comment)
tests/pi/obdii/test_obd_connection_bt.py (mocked BT path test, no real hardware)
specs/architecture.md (BT pairing flow paragraph)
docs/testing.md (CIO BT-pairing walkthrough)

groundingRefs.unit: default OBDLink LX BT pairing PIN (per OBDLink documentation; confirm with CIO)

acceptance: scripts/pair_obdlink.sh is a CIO-runnable bash driver that: (1) verifies bluetoothctl is available on the Pi, (2) scans for the OBDLink LX at the configured MAC, (3) initiates pairing (PIN-based if required), (4) sets up rfcomm binding to /dev/rfcomm0, (5) verifies binding survives a one-shot disconnect/reconnect, (6) writes BT MAC into the Pi's local .env OBD_BT_MAC if not already there
config.json pi.bluetooth.macAddress reads from ${OBD_BT_MAC} (already present per Sprint 11) â€” verify env var loads via secrets_loader
On the Pi: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'rfcomm show /dev/rfcomm0' returns the bound device with matching MAC

Pi smoke test: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 '~/obd2-venv/bin/python -c "import obd; c = obd.OBD(\"/dev/rfcomm0\", baudrate=38400); print(c.is_connected(), c.protocol_name())"' returns True + non-empty protocol name (likely ISO 9141-2 for the 1998 Eclipse)
Pairing survives Pi reboot: CIO reboots, waits ~60s, re-runs the smoke test, gets True. systemd-rfcomm or equivalent persists the binding (NOT manual rfcomm bind on every boot)

If BT pairing fails on first attempt, log captures bluetoothctl error stream verbatim â€” CIO can paste back to PM. Common failures: dongle not powered (engine off), wrong PIN, MAC typo, BT adapter disabled

Test: tests/pi/obdii/test_obd_connection_bt.py covers mocked BT path â€” connection-success, connection-timeout, BT-adapter-missing â€” without real hardware. Lets us regression-test on Windows + Pi without dongle present
Update specs/architecture.md Bluetooth section with the actual flow
Update docs/testing.md with a CIO 'how to re-pair after a battery yank' walkthrough
Completion note: bluetoothctl scan output, rfcomm bind output, smoke test output, reboot-survival evidence, any quirks (Eclipse ECU's protocol_name)
verification: ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'bluetoothctl info 00:04:3E:85:0D:FB' returns 'Paired: yes'
ssh mcornelison@10.27.27.28 'rfcomm show' shows /dev/rfcomm0 bound to correct MAC
Smoke test python -c (obd.OBD connection) returns True with car ignition ON
Same smoke test returns False (gracefully â€” no exception) with ignition OFF
pytest tests/pi/obdii/ -v on Windows + Pi (mocked tests, hardware-free)
ruff check src/pi/obdii/ scripts/pair_obdlink.sh tests/pi/obdii/
invariants: Do NOT hardcode the OBDLink MAC â€” config-driven via OBD_BT_MAC env var (B-044 standing rule)
Do NOT call sudo from Python â€” sudo-required commands stay in the bash pairing script
Do NOT auto-pair without CIO confirmation â€” pairing is a security event
Do NOT modify the obd.OBD() call signature â€” python-OBD's API is stable; if connection fails, fix config not the call
If the Pi's Bluetooth adapter is missing or disabled, surface to CIO with clear error â€” don't silently proceed
Document the protocol the Eclipse uses (likely ISO 9141-2 K-Line per specs/obd2-research.md) for Spool
stopConditions: STOP if bluetoothctl scan doesn't see the OBDLink LX after 30s â€” possible causes: (a) dongle not in pairing mode (some need a button press), (b) car ignition off (dongle is bus-powered), (c) BT range issue, (d) MAC typo. Surface bluetoothctl scan output to PM rather than guessing
STOP if pairing succeeds but rfcomm bind fails â€” log dmesg output (Bluetooth kernel module errors)
STOP if obd.OBD() connects but is_connected() returns False after 30s â€” protocol negotiation issue. Capture full debug log. May need explicit protocol= parameter
STOP if reboot-survival fails (binding gone after reboot) â€” needs systemd unit to re-bind on boot. Surface to PM, may want a follow-up TD
stories.completionNotes: Session 23 PM+CIO live drill — BT pair worked end-to-end. ENGINE OFF→KOEO→RUNNING. bluetoothctl pair via pexpect helper (ssp passkey auto-confirm); device Paired/Bonded/Trusted; rfcomm bind 0 00:04:3E:85:0D:FB 1 → /dev/rfcomm0; python-obd 0.7.3 → 'Car Connected | ISO 9141-2 | ELM327 v1.4b'. BENCH-PROVEN ACs (technical core): #2 OBD_BT_MAC env loads, #3 rfcomm show ok, #4 python-obd smoke True+ISO 9141-2. ENGINEERING DELIVERABLES CARRYFORWARD to Sprint 14 (NOT shipped this sprint): #1 scripts/pair_obdlink.sh in repo (exists on Pi at ~/Projects/Eclipse-01/scripts/ — Ralph to lift+commit), #5 reboot survival (rfcomm bind currently one-shot — needs systemd unit or /etc/bluetooth/rfcomm.conf), #7 tests/pi/obdii/test_obd_connection_bt.py mocked path, #8 specs/architecture.md BT flow update, #9 docs/testing.md walkthrough. Sprint passes:true on MILESTONE basis; carryforward filed in Ralph inbox.

stories.title: Live idle data verification â€” first real OBD reads
stories.intent: With the engine running at idle in the garage and US-167 paired, query the Eclipse's ECU. Capture 5+ readings of core PIDs, validate against grounded-knowledge ranges. Decode VIN. Data lands in Pi SQLite. This proves the FULL real-data path works for the first time.

stories.completionNotes: Session 23 PM+CIO live drill — first real Eclipse OBD-II data EVER persisted to local SQLite. 5-sweep cold-start manual query (RPM 1187-1214 fast-idle, coolant 23→26°C climbing, STFT +2.34%, timing 14-17°). Then production orchestrator src/pi/main.py ran 60s headless (SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy due to TD-024). RESULT: 149 rows in data/obd.db across 11 PIDs in 60s (~2.5 readings/sec — matches K-line tier-1 polling spec). ECU witnessed cold→warm closed-loop transition: RPM 793 avg, coolant 73-74°C steady (~165°F), LTFT=0.00% across all 13 samples (tune dialed), STFT -0.78 to +1.56% oscillating, O2 0V↔0.82V switching (closed-loop), timing 5-9° (closed-loop idle), MAF 3.5gps warm vs 6gps cold, intake 14°C ambient, throttle 0.78% closed, speed 0. CONFIRMED PIDs not supported on stock 2G ECU: Fuel Pressure (0x0A), MAP (0x0B), Control Module Voltage (0x42) — matches obd2-research.md. BENCH-PROVEN ACs: #2 ranges valid, #4 speed=0 sanity, #6 SQLite count > 30 (got 149). CARRYFORWARD to Sprint 14: #1 scripts/verify_live_idle.sh in repo, #3 VIN decode (didn't run separately), #5 supported-PIDs file, #7 regression fixture export, #8 range-check test file, #9 grounded-knowledge.md measured values, #10 full completion-note doc. Sprint passes:true on MILESTONE basis; carryforward filed in Ralph inbox.

stories.blockedReason: TD-024 — pi.hardware.status_display crashes with 'Could not make GL context current: BadAccess (attempt to access private resource denied)' when launched under X11 (DISPLAY=:0 XAUTHORITY=$HOME/.Xauthority SDL_VIDEODRIVER=x11). Crash propagates and kills orchestrator runLoop at uptime=0.6s. Headless (SDL_VIDEODRIVER=dummy) avoids the crash but loses HDMI render. pi.hardware.status_display is a separate hardware-monitor overlay distinct from pi.display.manager (the primary OSOYOO render). Primary display proven working under X11 in Session 22 via render_primary_screen_live.py. Path to unblock: triage status_display GL usage (does it need GL accel? can it use software renderer?), or disable status_display via config when DISPLAY is X11 with no GL grant. DEFERRED to Sprint 14 — TD-024 + new story US-192 (US-170 retry post-TD-024 fix).

stories.title: WiFi / home-network detection (B-043 building block)
stories.intent: Bench-only foundation work for B-043 auto-sync. Build a small module that answers 'are we on DeathStarWiFi?' and 'can we reach Chi-Srv-01?' Returns a HomeNetworkState enum. Logged on transitions. Used by the future PowerLossOrchestrator (US-189, Sprint 14). NO orchestration logic in this story â€” just detection.
stories.completionNotes: Session 56. New: src/pi/network/ package with __init__.py + home_detector.py (HomeNetworkDetector class + HomeNetworkState StrEnum + _readSsidViaIwgetid + _readLocalIps default helpers). Constructor takes DI seams (ssidReader, ipReader, httpOpener, apiKey) so entire module is Windows-testable without iwgetid or hostname binaries. Four-state composition exactly matches AC: AT_HOME_SERVER_REACHABLE / AT_HOME_SERVER_DOWN / AWAY / UNKNOWN. Defense in depth honored â€” isAtHomeWifi() requires BOTH SSID == config-ssid AND at-least-one IP in config-subnet. iwgetid infra-missing (FileNotFoundError / TimeoutExpired / OSError) maps to None -> UNKNOWN; iwgetid ran but non-zero (not connected to any WiFi) maps to empty string -> AWAY. isServerReachable does GET {baseUrl}{serverPingPath} with X-API-Key header + bounded timeout, never raises (HTTPError/URLError/TimeoutError/OSError all -> False). Transition logging per AC: first call records baseline silently, subsequent state changes emit INFO with old + new state names, same-state polling is silent. Tests: tests/pi/network/test_home_detector.py = 30 tests across 6 test classes (state branches, both-checks-required defense in depth, isServerReachable error paths + timeout flow, transition logging first/change/same, subprocess helpers via subprocess.run patch, config-driven overrides incl. invalid-CIDR graceful degrade). Validator wiring: src/common/config/validator.py DEFAULTS gained 4 pi.homeNetwork keys (ssid/subnet/pingTimeoutSeconds/serverPingPath) + new _validateHomeNetwork method enforcing non-empty SSID, valid CIDR (ipaddress.ip_network), positive numeric pingTimeoutSeconds with bool-first guard (matches companion-service pattern), absolute-path serverPingPath. tests/test_config_validator.py gained TestHomeNetworkConfig class (7 tests: defaults applied, round-trip, empty SSID, invalid CIDR, zero timeout, bool timeout, relative path). config.json gained pi.homeNetwork section (rest of live config unchanged). specs/architecture.md Configuration Sections table gained pi.homeNetwork row + new #### section documenting detector semantics + defense-in-depth invariant. Quality: tests/pi/network/ = 30/30 passed in 34s. tests/test_config_validator.py = 70/70 (was 63 + 7 new). Full suite pytest tests/ -q = 2182 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed in 635s (baseline 2145 + 37 new = 2182 exact). ruff check src/ tests/ clean. python validate_config.py clean. Ready for US-189 PowerLossOrchestrator in Sprint 14 to subscribe and branch on HomeNetworkState.

acceptance: src/pi/network/home_detector.py exposes: HomeNetworkState enum (AT_HOME_SERVER_REACHABLE, AT_HOME_SERVER_DOWN, AWAY, UNKNOWN), isAtHomeWifi() -> bool, isServerReachable() -> bool, getHomeNetworkState() -> HomeNetworkState
isAtHomeWifi() checks BOTH SSID match (via 'iwgetid -r') AND subnet match (via Pi's IP in 10.27.27.0/24). True only if both pass â€” defense in depth
isServerReachable() does GET {baseUrl}/api/v1/ping with X-API-Key header + bounded timeout (default 3s). Returns True on 2xx, False otherwise. Does NOT raise on network errors

getHomeNetworkState() composes the two: at home + server up â†’ AT_HOME_SERVER_REACHABLE, at home + server down â†’ AT_HOME_SERVER_DOWN, not at home â†’ AWAY. UNKNOWN if SSID detection fails (no WiFi adapter)

Config defaults in pi.homeNetwork: ssid='DeathStarWiFi', subnet='10.27.27.0/24', pingTimeoutSeconds=3, serverPingPath='/api/v1/ping'. ConfigValidator enforces shape (string SSID, valid CIDR, positive timeout)
Tests cover: at-home-server-up, at-home-server-down, away (SSID mismatch), away (no WiFi adapter â€” iwgetid fails), unknown (subprocess timeout). 5+ tests minimum
Logging: state changes from one HomeNetworkState to another get logged at INFO level with both old and new state
Completion note: state-table sample for each scenario (mocked), test counts, ruff result. Does NOT need live-Pi verification â€” that's B-043 follow-on (US-189)

Do NOT block on subprocess â€” bounded timeouts on every shell-out
Do NOT make this module import any Pi-only hardware libs â€” must be testable on Windows (mocking subprocess)
Do NOT use SyncClient for the ping check â€” circular dep. Use plain urllib or requests with bounded timeout

STOP if test ergonomics get ugly because subprocess-mocking is hard â€” propose redesign (e.g., dependency-injected detector functions) before going down a rabbit hole

SCP a known SQLite fixture to the Pi â†’ run sync_now.py â†’ assert exact server row counts. Replaces the broken validate_pi_to_server.sh sim-launch path. Closes B-045.
stories.completionNotes: Session 57. Five deliverables: (1) scripts/seed_pi_fixture.py â€” standalone deterministic Pi-fixture builder (481 LOC). Full 11-table Pi schema via ALL_SCHEMAS/ALL_INDEXES + sync_log.initDb. 3 canonical specs in FIXTURES dict: cold_start (1 drive Ã— 300s @ 10s Ã— 5 params = 150 realtime), local_loop (1 Ã— 900s @ 10s Ã— 10 params = 900), errand_day (3 Ã— 360/720/360s @ 3s Ã— 5 params = 2400). Pure closed-form synthesis (math.sin with per-drive phase offset; no random/time.now); VACUUM at finish + sorted sqlite_sequence rewrite â†’ bit-for-bit reproducible (SHA256 match 3/3 across two consecutive full --all runs, verified in tests). Per-param _BASELINE dict with (center, amplitude, period) tuned so generated values land inside grounded-knowledge ranges. Profile + vehicle_info rows pre-seeded (Eclipse VIN 4A3AK54F8WE122916, profile 'daily'); ai_recommendations/alert_log/calibration_sessions intentionally empty placeholders so SyncClient's 8-table iteration works. sync_log explicitly seeded with one row per IN_SCOPE_TABLES entry at last_synced_id=0 status='pending' (equivalent to empty via getHighWaterMark default but self-documenting). CLI: --fixture NAME + --output, OR --all + --output-dir. Returns 2 on missing-required-arg. (2) data/regression/pi-inputs/{cold_start,local_loop,errand_day}.db checked into git (155KB/266KB/458KB). (3) scripts/replay_pi_fixture.sh â€” 8-step bash driver (390 LOC). Positional fixture name OR --fixture flag, --dry-run, --keep-service-stopped, --help/-h. Steps: SSH gates â†’ stop eclipse-obd â†’ read local fixture counts (authoritative expected deltas) â†’ query server pre-counts via ssh_server + mysql.connector one-liner â†’ SCP fixture to PI_PATH/data/obd.db + rm stale .db-shm/.db-wal â†’ ssh run sync_now.py (grep for Status: OK/DISABLED, fail on FAILED) â†’ server post-counts â†’ assert per-table delta == fixture EXACTLY (tabular output with 'PASS'/'FAIL' column) â†’ summary + optional service restart. Non-numeric server-count sentinels (SERVER_QUERY_FAIL, NO_MYSQL_CONNECTOR, TABLE_ERROR:*) short-circuit to FAIL without arithmetic. Exit 0/1/2 per AC. Overrides via deploy/deploy.conf. (4) tests/scripts/test_seed_pi_fixture.py = 20 tests across 8 classes â€” row-count exactness for all 3 fixtures, SHA256 determinism (parametrized across 3), 12-table schema completeness (incl. sync_log), sync_log per-IN_SCOPE_TABLES seeding, profile + VIN baseline rows, drive shape (3 starts+ends for errand_day, 1 for cold_start), stats sample_count mirrors realtime COUNT per param, FixtureSpec length validation, CLI parse-error exit codes, end-to-end main() integration. (5) tests/scripts/test_replay_pi_fixture_sh.py = 13 tests across 5 classes â€” --help/-h, --dry-run 8-step ordering (walk-through assertion), positional-vs-flag equivalence, --keep-service-stopped short-circuit, 4 exit-2 error paths (missing fixture / unknown flag / duplicate positional / nonexistent-fixture-without-dry-run), driver-file shebang sanity. _skipWithoutBash skipif keeps the suite green if bash vanishes from PATH. subprocess.run(timeout=60) with bash itself (cheap) â€” no Windows-Store-Python cold-start flake. Additional: validate_pi_to_server.sh got DEPRECATED header + --fixture flag + Step 1-3 rewired to delegate to replay_pi_fixture.sh (physics-sim launch path gone; --duration accepted but ignored for backward compat); src/pi/obdii/simulator/README.md created with 'Status: Deprecated for Testing' banner + canonical-replacement table + file map; docs/testing.md Walk-Phase section replaced with Flat-File Replay Validation section (fixture-regeneration commands, fixture table, per-step-proves table, live-run prerequisites). Quality: tests/scripts/ = 47/47 (20 new seed + 13 new replay + 14 existing sync_now). Full suite pytest tests/ -q = 2215 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed in 661s (baseline 2182 + 33 new = 2215 exact, zero regressions). ruff check src/ tests/ clean. Exact-delta assertions proven via the test suite (row counts match spec AC of 150/900/2400 exactly). Ready for CIO to run bash scripts/replay_pi_fixture.sh cold_start on chi-eclipse-01 + chi-srv-01 bench for live-fire validation. Sprint 13 is now 2/5 passes:true (US-188 + US-191). Remaining 3 all need CIO+car: US-167 gateway, US-168 live idle, US-170 display. All solo-windows-dev work exhausted.

acceptance: scripts/replay_pi_fixture.sh accepts a fixture name and: (1) SSH to Pi, (2) sudo systemctl stop eclipse-obd (no producer during test), (3) capture pre-sync server row counts via /api/v1/health or small admin endpoint, (4) SCP data/regression/pi-inputs/<fixture>.db to ~/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db (overwrite), (5) ssh + run sync_now.py, (6) capture post-sync server row counts, (7) assert delta == fixture row count EXACTLY (per-table), (8) print PASS/FAIL summary, exit 0/1

data/regression/pi-inputs/ has 3+ deterministic fixtures: cold_start.db (~5min, ~150 realtime rows), local_loop.db (~15min, ~900 rows), errand_day.db (~3 short drives + parked gaps, ~2000 rows). All fixtures include realtime_data + connection_log + statistics + sync_log (last_synced_id=0). Empty placeholder tables for the other 5 in-scope tables so SyncClient doesn't choke (today's gotcha)
Each fixture is bit-for-bit reproducible â€” re-running the seed script generates identical bytes (deterministic seed, no time.now() in the data)
